refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined productivity target by optimizing equipment operation and task execution efficiency.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and outcome-focused; refining it into sub-FRs clarifies specific functional aspects that contribute to productivity, enabling targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure productivity metrics (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations and verify that sub-functions contribute to meeting or exceeding the target.
sub_frs: FR1: Optimize equipment operational parameters to maximize throughput.
FR2: Efficiently schedule and execute worksite tasks to minimize idle time.
FR3: Provide real-time performance feedback to operators and tele-operators.

candidate_dps: DP1: Adaptive equipment control algorithms for operational optimization.
DP2: Task scheduling and dispatch system integrated with worksite management.
DP3: Real-time telemetry and feedback interface for operator awareness.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Adaptive equipment control algorithms for operational optimization.

potential_sources: Interaction between control algorithms and task scheduling
Dependency on telemetry data for feedback
coupling_check.notes: DP1 is logically separable but requires well-defined interfaces with DP2 and DP3 to avoid tight coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate sensor data for control algorithms.
Risk of performance degradation if control algorithms are not well integrated with task scheduling.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider modularizing control and scheduling functions to reduce coupling and improve maintainability.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and embedded control systems responsible for equipment operational parameters and control logic.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends operational data and status to other modules such as Teleoperation and Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task execution commands and schedules from higher-level modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of control logic within Vehicle; low diffusion as control algorithms are centralized.
Broader scope; control logic would be diffused if placed here.
Not suitable; control logic unrelated to teleoperation functions.
No containment; safety services focus on alerts and monitoring.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Control algorithms for operational optimization are best contained within the Vehicle module to ensure tight integration with embedded systems and sensors.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules.
Risk of insufficient modularity if control logic grows in complexity.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts and consider splitting control logic if complexity increases.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (adaptive control algorithms) directly addresses the refined FR of optimizing equipment operation to meet productivity targets.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as control logic is centralized in Vehicle module.
Interfaces with Tasking and Telemetry are well-defined.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Vehicle as the best IM boundary for the selected DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring productivity metrics and validating control algorithm performance within the Vehicle module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of control logic to Vehicle module with defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Monitor interface complexity between Vehicle and other modules during integration.
Prepare for potential modularization if control logic scope expands.

decision.justification: The current level of functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment satisfy the criteria for progressing. Interfaces and containment are well defined with manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Optimize equipment operational parameters to maximize throughput.
FR2: Efficiently schedule and execute worksite tasks to minimize idle time.
FR3: Provide real-time performance feedback to operators and tele-operators.

revise.why: No immediate revisions required; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Sensor data is reliable and timely for control algorithms.
Tasking commands are accurately received by Vehicle module.
open_risks: Interface complexity may increase with system scale.
Potential need for modularization if control logic complexity grows.
evidence_needed: Performance data from prototype Vehicle control algorithms.
Interface validation tests between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
